status_detail: 1463B envelope (deepseek_exp_2k) did NOT render warning text. UI showed 'network error' instead. completion returned 200/h2, no Console errors (no INVALID_JSON, no ERR_HTTP2). Response received but frontend could not parse/render it.

test_results.name: password keyword warning test (1463B envelope)
test_results.expected: ERR_HTTP2 expected if 500B ceiling is real
test_results.actual: UI showed: '네트워크를 확인하고 다시 시도하세요.' (Check your network and try again)
test_results.notes: No ERR_HTTP2 protocol error. No INVALID_JSON in Console. completion returned 200/h2. But frontend rendered network error — likely SSE body too large for DeepSeek Lyla parser to process correctly.

experiment_summary.step2_476B: PARTIAL — blank chat, SSE silently consumed
experiment_summary.step4_1463B: FAIL — network error, no protocol/parse errors in Console
experiment_summary.conclusion: Sweet spot is around 935B. Both smaller (249B) and larger (1463B) envelopes fail. The deepseek_exp_1k template at 935B is the only one that successfully renders warning text. There appears to be both a lower bound (SSE format must be valid enough) and an upper bound (response body must not exceed some internal limit).

console_errors: Console cleared before test. No new errors appeared after message sent.
network_observations: completion 200/h2, list 200/ht, no failed requests in Network tab
